commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow repair can fix shared-client scoped-store ownership and add the missing two-store regression without a product decision.; Needs attention: The diff still has a concrete credential-routing concern because scoped OAuth refresh state can be captured by a reused shared client.; Review finding: [P1] Separate scoped stores before reusing shared clients — extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts:259 (sha=5c49855b7be4e575eeb14ee2f6a833233528cbbf)
commands.review_summary:
Threads scoped auth profile stores through shared Codex app-server startup and OAuth refresh paths, with focused regression coverage for shared refresh, startup factory forwarding, and turn-watch behavior.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +98. Total +120 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows bound Codex conversations use leased shared clients, current main lacks a shared refresh handler, and upstream Codex requires an accessToken response; I did not rerun live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can fix shared-client scoped-store ownership and add the missing two-store regression without a product decision.
